title: Moussa Diabate Wins 2025-26 NBA Hustle Award
summary: Charlotte Hornets center Moussa Diabate has won the 2025-26 NBA Hustle Award. The honor recognizes players whose effort-based contributions drive winning but rarely appear in traditional statistical categories. Diabate's impact coincided directly with Charlotte's dramatic midseason turnaround. The Hornets went 9-20 through Dec. 22 before posting a 35-18 record the rest of the way, the sixth-best winning percentage in the league during that stretch. Dec. 23 marked Diabate's first game as a full-time starter. The award is determined by a statistical formula tracking deflections, loose balls recovered, charges drawn, screen assists, contested shots, and box outs. Among the 124 eligible players, Diabate ranked first in offensive box outs per minute and fourth in screen assists per minute. Diabate posted career-best numbers across the board in his fourth NBA season, averaging 7.9 points on 63.1 percent shooting, 8.7 rebounds, 1.9 assists, 1.0 blocks and 0.8 steals in 26.0 minutes per game. His 20 double-doubles nearly tripled his previous career total. Dyson Daniels, Draymond Green, Cedric Coward and Josh Hart rounded out the top-5.

title: Caitlin Clark Exits Fever Preseason Game With Right Ankle Injury After Hard Landing
summary: Indiana Fever guard Caitlin Clark left Thursday's preseason game against the Dallas Wings with a right ankle injury in the third quarter. Clark landed on the foot of Dallas forward Alanna Smith after attempting a stepback three-pointer and immediately showed signs of pain. Officials reviewed the play and assessed Smith, the reigning co-Defensive Player of the Year, a flagrant foul for failing to give Clark adequate landing space. Clark converted two of three free throws before departing at the 7:51 mark of the third quarter. The two-time All-Star was examined by Indiana's training staff on the bench but did not go to the locker room. She finished with 21 points on 4-of-6 shooting, converting 11 of 13 free throws in 16 minutes of action. Clark also recorded four assists and two rebounds. The injury adds concern for a player already managing a fragile health history. Clark appeared in just 13 games during her 2025 sophomore season due to injury. Thursday marked only her second preseason appearance, following her return against the New York Liberty last Saturday.

title: NBA Urges Federal Regulator To Tighten Prediction Market Rules To Protect Game Integrity
summary: The NBA formally asked the Commodity Futures Trading Commission on Thursday to impose stricter regulations on prediction markets, citing risks to competitive integrity and public confidence in professional sports. The league's submission arrived on the final day of the CFTC's public comment period, which drew nearly 1,500 responses since opening March 13. Dan Spillane, the NBA's executive vice president and assistant general counsel, authored the letter. He argued that prediction markets pose integrity concerns comparable to traditional sports betting, particularly when players, game officials, or team staff can trade contracts tied to competitions in which they participate. "There is no higher priority for the NBA than protecting the integrity of our games and preserving public confidence in our league and in our sport," Spillane wrote. The NBA's position stands apart from several peer leagues. MLB, the NHL, and the UFC have each signed partnership agreements with prediction market platforms rather than seeking tighter federal oversight. Spillane flagged specific market categories as vulnerable to manipulation, including those tied to officiating decisions, player injuries, and fan conduct. The letter also called for a minimum trading age of 21, stricter than the current 18-and-older threshold used by platforms such as Kalshi and Polymarket. The league stopped short of taking sides in the ongoing jurisdictional dispute between individual states and the CFTC over regulatory authority. Additional requests included improved data sharing between prediction market operators and leagues, along with stronger cooperation on suspicious trading activity and internal league investigations.

title: Lakers Relocate G League Team To Coachella Valley
summary: The Los Angeles Lakers are relocating their NBA G League team to the Greater Palm Springs region in California, where it will be renamed the Coachella Valley Lakers. Beginning with the 2026-27 NBA G League season, the Coachella Valley Lakers will play their home games at Acrisure Arena in Palm Desert. The Lakers have long played in El Segundo. "I have enjoyed a long-time relationship with Jerry and Jeanie Buss, Lon Rosen and Linda Rambis," said Oak View Group Senior Partner Irving Azoff. "And I am beyond thrilled to have the Coachella Valley Lakers call OVG's Acrisure Arena their home. Go Lakers." "Moving the Lakers G League team to the Coachella Valley is an incredible opportunity for the organization," said Los Angeles Lakers President of Business Operations Lon Rosen. "The Lakers have had a strong presence in the region for decades, from the Showtime Lakers holding training camp in the 1980s to more recent preseason games. We are looking forward to extending that experience and becoming a staple for Coachella Valley sports and entertainment. Acrisure Arena is the perfect modern venue that provides an incredible fan-first experience, while ensuring players have the premium facilities and space they need on game day."

title: Rockets Avoid Elimination Again, Force Game 6 Against Lakers
summary: The Houston Rockets avoided elimination Wednesday night, defeating the Los Angeles Lakers 99-93 in Game 5 to cut the first-round series deficit to 3-2. Houston becomes the 16th NBA team to force a Game 6 after falling behind 3-0. Reed Sheppard delivered key plays in the closing minutes. With Houston ahead by three and under three minutes remaining, he hit a midrange jumper and then stripped LeBron James for a dunk, pushing the lead to seven. "We definitely remember what happened in Game 3, and we didn't want to let that happen again," said Sheppard, who posted 12 points and six assists while battling illness. Jabari Smith Jr. led Houston with 22 points and seven rebounds. A day before the game, with the Rockets down 3-1, he told reporters, "We're obviously the better team, I feel like." Smith backed up that claim on the court Wednesday. "You can't just say it and then come out and tuck your tail. You got to get out there and fight," Smith said. The victory is notable given that Kevin Durant has missed four of five games due to injury. Houston has outscored Los Angeles 514-509 across the series. James paced the Lakers with 25 points and seven assists in defeat. Los Angeles committed 15 turnovers, four more than Houston. "Some of [our mistakes] were game-plan discipline, and some of them were just unforced errors," James said. "So, we got to be better in both areas." Ime Udoka credited team development after his club's late-game collapse in Game 3 prompted sharp public criticism. Game 6 is set for Friday in Houston with the Rockets favored by 3.5 points . The Lakers, however, remain -295 favorites and a 75 percent probability to win the series.

title: Kenny Atkinson Credits 'Dennis The Menace' For Game 5 Win
summary: The Cleveland Cavaliers defeated the Toronto Raptors 125-120 in Game 5 on Wednesday to take a 3-2 series lead. Dennis Schroder scored 11 of his 19 points in the fourth quarter while also playing the entire quarter. "We needed to win this game," Schroder said. "It's all about momentum. They had the momentum going into Game 5 because they won two in a row. We played in front of our fans and we didn't play how we should play." The Cavs entered the fourth quarter down 103-100. It was the first Cavs' playoff win when being down to start the fourth quarter since 2018, also against the Raptors. "When we were talking about trading for [Schroder], that was one of the thing that came up: This guy has played in huge games in the NBA and then on the international scene," Cavs coach Kenny Atkinson said. summary: The Detroit Pistons defeated the Orlando Magic 116-109 in Game 5 on Wednesday night to stave off elimination. Both Cade Cunningham and Paolo Banchero scored 45 points in the game. It was the second time in playoff history where two opposing players scored 45 points, with Donovan Mitchell scoring 51 points and Jamal Murray scoring 45 points in 2020. "Not everybody is blessed with those types of opportunities to have pressure and things on the line like that," said Cunningham after the game. "A lot of times I feel undeserving of how great of moments I get to be a part of. Just try to make the most of it." Playing without Franz Wagner, Banchero finished 17-of-31 from the field and hit all six 3s. "In the heat of the moment, you're not really thinking about it," Banchero said of the duel. "You're just thinking about getting the win. But sure, one day [I'll] look back and be like, 'That was a hell of a game.' But me and him have been going at it since AAU days. So no surprise there that happened." Pistons coach J.B. Bickerstaff complimented his team's physicality in the win. "We come out swinging, we come out kicking, we come out scratching, biting, clawing, whatever's necessary to get it done," Bickerstaff said. "And that spirit carries over. And the want to not let your teammate down carries over." With the Magic up 3-2 in the series, Game 6 takes place on Friday at Orlando with the Pistons favored by 3.5 points to force a Game 7 .

title: Steve Kerr, Warriors Hold Two-Hour Meeting With No Resolution On Coaching Future
summary: Golden State Warriors head coach Steve Kerr met with controlling owner Joe Lacob and general manager Mike Dunleavy for two hours Monday, yet the two-week-old question of whether Kerr will return next season remains unresolved, according to ESPN sources. The parties plan to meet again next week. Both sides characterized the session as constructive. Kerr is described by those close to him as genuinely conflicted about his own decision, and will depart on a previously scheduled golf trip before talks resume. "It's April," one source said. "We don't need to rush." The draft lottery on May 10 represents the first significant offseason milestone. Golden State holds the 11th slot, carrying a 9.4% chance of moving into the top four and a 77.6% chance of remaining at 11. Central to Kerr's deliberations is whether the franchise can meaningfully upgrade its roster this summer. Sources indicate there is broad agreement that Kerr remains a strong fit leading a legitimate playoff contender built around Stephen Curry and Draymond Green, but not necessarily the right choice to spearhead a full organizational rebuild. "I still love coaching, but I get it," Kerr said the night Golden State was eliminated. "These jobs all have an expiration date. There is a run that happens, and when the run ends, sometimes it's time for new blood and new ideas." Team sources insist the deliberations are purely a basketball decision, centered on Kerr's coaching appetite, potential offensive philosophy adjustments and roster-building confidence heading into a competitive Western Conference. Warriors management has not initiated a coaching search as that process would only begin if Kerr chooses to step away.

title: Caleb Wilson Increasingly Viewed By NBA Scouts In Same Tier As Dybantsa, Peterson, Boozer
summary: North Carolina forward Caleb Wilson is slotted at fourth overall in Sam Vecenie's latest NBA mock draft for The Athletic, buoyed by strong head-to-head performances against his top rivals despite missing the final month of the season with a broken right thumb and broken left hand. Before the injuries, Wilson averaged 19.8 points, 9.4 rebounds, 2.7 assists, 1.5 steals and 1.4 blocks per game. Scouts are increasingly placing him alongside AJ Dybantsa, Darryn Peterson and Cameron Boozer as a legitimate top-three candidate in the class ahead of one or more of the other three prospects at the top of draft boards. Wilson's physical profile drives the evaluation. His combination of body strength, leaping ability and low-center-of-gravity driving technique makes him a punishing finisher at the rim. Vecenie draws a direct comparison to Pascal Siakam, a multiple All-NBA selection. Concerns exist, however. Wilson attempted just 27 three-pointers all season, converting only seven, though his mechanics from extended midrange range drew positive reviews. Defensively, his block and steal totals are impressive, but scouts note he lacks the lateral quickness expected of an athlete his size and struggles with off-ball rotations and reaction time. Wilson's most compelling asset, evaluators say, remains his relentless motor and physical aggression, qualities that proved reliable throughout the season and few prospects at any level consistently demonstrate.

title: Every NBA Champion Since 2015 Has Won First Round In Five Or Fewer Games
summary: The Oklahoma City Thunder swept the Phoenix Suns in the first round of the 2026 NBA Playoffs as they look to extend a remarkable 11-year pattern in which every NBA champion has advanced past the first round in five or fewer games. The streak dates to the Golden State Warriors' title run in 2015. Oklahoma City also swept the Memphis Grizzlies in last season's first round en route to the championship, making the Thunder just the sixth team to sweep the opening round and go on to win the title since the NBA expanded to a best-of-seven first round format in 2003. NBA Champions With First Round Sweep • 2013: Miami Heat (Bucks) • 2015: Golden State Warriors (Pelicans) • 2016: Cleveland Cavaliers (Pistons) • 2017: Golden State Warriors (Blazers) • 2021: Milwaukee Bucks (Heat) • 2025: Oklahoma City Thunder (Grizzlies) The San Antonio Spurs have the second best odds of winning the NBA championship and eliminated the Portland Trail Blazers in their first round series in five games, which keeps them within the historical pattern. Since 2003, seventeen champions have exited the first round with one loss or fewer, representing a 74 percent success rate. Only the 2008 Boston Celtics and 2014 Spurs survived seven-game first-round series before ultimately winning the title. Historically, champions exiting the first round with minimal resistance have proven the rule rather than the exception as they immediately exert their dominance at the start of the playoffs. title: AJ Dybantsa Converts Nike NIL Deal Into Pro Contract
summary: BYU freshman AJ Dybantsa is converting his existing Nike NIL agreement into a professional endorsement contract as he prepares for the 2026 NBA Draft, he confirmed to ESPN. The 6-foot-9 forward is widely projected as a top pick in June . "It means everything," Dybantsa said. "They've been with me since Day 1. It's been a great partnership and we're looking forward to keep building for the long term." Nike also unveiled a personal logo for Dybantsa, a star composed of his initials, tied to the new deal. The design references his childhood nickname, "Star Boy." Dybantsa led the nation in scoring during his lone college season, averaging 25.5 points per game on 51% shooting, with 6.8 rebounds and 3.7 assists at BYU. He has been training in Los Angeles alongside former Boston Celtics guard Isaiah Thomas and trainer Zack Gonzales. "Ever since the rankings came out in 9th grade, I've been No. 1 [in my class]," Dybantsa told ESPN. "I haven't been nothing lower. So, it's definitely a goal of mine to go No. 1." The Boston native is also adjusting to the extended NBA three-point line after shooting 33.1% from deep in college. He attended Game 2 of the Lakers-Rockets playoff series to observe the professional game firsthand. BYU's strength staff compared Dybantsa's athletic metrics directly to current NBA players, while head coach Kevin Young, a former Philadelphia 76ers and Phoenix Suns assistant, emphasized pro-style schemes throughout the season. Beyond basketball, Dybantsa told ESPN he intends to establish charitable roots in whichever city selects him, drawing inspiration from his father's philanthropic work in the Republic of the Congo. He also made his immediate competitive ambitions clear. "There's so many greats that have won Rookie of the Year ," he said. "I don't want to not win Rookie of the Year."

Bonga has appeared in two Olympics, the FIBA World Cup and EuroBasket, as well as qualifiers for the aforementioned tournaments for Germany. Several NBA teams reached out to Partizan about Bonga's availability midseason, but the Serbian club held off interest. Bonga has a buyout of $875,000 this offseason that would allow him to join an NBA team.

title: LeBron James Addresses Michael Jordan GOAT Debate, Finals Record In Wide-Ranging Interview
summary: LeBron James, 41, spoke candidly about his relationship with Michael Jordan's legacy in a series of ESPN interviews conducted throughout the season. The Los Angeles Lakers star addressed the GOAT debate, stylistic differences between the two players, and his 4-6 Finals record. The interviews, conducted by ESPN's Dave McMenamin across eight cities over the course of the year, capture James near the conclusion of his NBA career. "I hope I made him proud at least, wearing that No. 23," James told McMenamin. James, listed at 6-foot-9 and 250 pounds, was direct about the stylistic contrast between himself and Jordan, who played at 6-6 and 200 pounds. "I never have compared myself to MJ because our games are totally different," James said. "I have been a point-forward/forward-point my whole life. I have always looked for the pass. MJ kind of looked for the shot. Not kind of, he did. He looked for the shot. There are a lot of things where I would say my game is a lot different and a little better than his, but s---, he was f---ing great. We're both great. We're both great basketball players." James credited Jordan as a formative influence during his childhood in Akron, Ohio. "I never thought that I could be him, but I dreamed of the opportunity to live in the shoes that he lived in," James said. "I dreamed of being able to be in a big game and hit a game winner as the clock went down. I dreamed of having my own sneaker. I dreamed of flying through the air like him. I dreamed of people screaming my name. Everything that he did. I needed inspiration from certain people in music and in sports during my upbringing. I needed that." On his Finals record, James pushed back against critics who use it to diminish his career. "I am pissed off that I didn't have a better winning percentage individually in the Finals, but for people to try to turn it into a negative, it doesn't bother me like it did when I was younger," James said.
